decisionPanels.title: 🎙️ Audio +45% YoY — Guardian's podcast push is structurally vindicated
decisionPanels.summary: Backlight's 2026 Media Stats Report (now confirmed across BroadcastBeat, 4rfv, and BroadcastBeat; March 10-12) reveals audio assets grew 45% year-on-year across its platform — driven by podcasts, voiceovers, music libraries, and multilingual audio tracks. This is the fastest-growing asset type after vertical video. The Guardian launched its flagship video podcast in February and just formed Guardian Studios. The infrastructure timing is right: MMR's LucidLink + iconik stack must handle audio-first asset management at scale, not as an afterthought. Multilingual audio tracks in particular signal the direction of global audience growth. 903 million total assets and 324 PB storage now tracked across Backlight's platform — Guardian is a small but growing part of that ecosystem.
actions: Audit MMR iconik metadata schema for audio assets: does it support podcast-specific fields (episode number, series, language, transcript attachment)?
Ensure Project Launcher covers audio production workflows — not just video. Guardian Studios will need audio collections in iconik from day one.
Flag to multimedia leadership: audio +45% is a platform-wide trend. Budget and capacity planning for audio storage in LucidLink should reflect this growth rate.

decisionPanels.title: ☁️ LucidLink Connect GA: Guardian archive activation — no migration needed
decisionPanels.summary: LucidLink Connect is now generally available (SiliconANGLE exclusive March 5; Blocks & Files deep-dive March 10; SVG Europe March 6). The confirmed capability: existing AWS S3 buckets can be streamed directly into LucidLink Filespaces without ingesting or moving data. Native file formats preserved. SOC 2 Type II and MPA TPN Blue Shield certified. CEO Peter Thompson: 'activate existing files without ingesting, without moving them.' TeamCache (early access) reduces bandwidth for office-colocated teams. CLI linking confirmed for a future release. If Guardian has any media archive on AWS S3, this is immediately actionable — no migration project required. Available now on Enterprise plans.
actions: Ask Guardian infrastructure/cloud team: does Guardian have existing media archives on AWS S3? If yes, LucidLink Connect (Enterprise) can activate them without migration.
If S3 archives exist, evaluate LucidLink Enterprise plan and Connect as bridge between legacy archive and MMR's live production Filespace.
Flag TeamCache early access to LucidLink account team — if multimedia team is office-colocated, this reduces bandwidth overhead significantly.

decisionPanels.title: 🎬 AWS Elemental Inference + UK video ads £9.3bn — live vertical publishing is now an infrastructure decision
decisionPanels.summary: Two signals this week that together define the stakes. First: AWS launched Elemental Inference (Hollywood Reporter, ~2 weeks ago) — AI-powered real-time vertical video conversion from live and on-demand feeds with 6-10 second latency. Uses saliency mapping, object tracking, and speaker detection to auto-frame 9:16 from 16:9 during live broadcasts. Fox Sports and NBCUniversal are already using it. This means Guardian could publish live 9:16 clips to TikTok and Instagram *during* breaking news events — press conferences, verdicts, elections — with no post-production. Second: IAB UK reports UK digital video ad investment hit £9.3 billion in 2025, +20% year-on-year, now 24% of all UK digital ad spend. CTV = 34% of video. 57% of respondents expect budgets higher in 2026. The commercial case for Guardian video is rock solid — the infrastructure gap is the only constraint.
actions: Evaluate AWS Elemental Inference for Guardian's live news vertical publishing — particularly for breaking news, elections, and live events coverage.
Ensure MMR Project Launcher metadata templates treat vertical (9:16) as a first-class asset type with its own delivery destination fields.
Brief Guardian Studios on IAB UK data: video is 24% of UK digital ad spend — this is the commercial argument for investing in Guardian Studios infrastructure now.

decisionPanels.title: ⚖️ UK AI copyright delayed + BBC Sport digital-first — two signals shaping Guardian's 2026 position
decisionPanels.summary: Two important context signals. First: the UK Government 'kicked the can down the road' on AI copyright rule changes (VideoWeek, March 6). This is good news for Guardian's SPUR coalition — the regulatory pressure hasn't eased, but Guardian is ahead of the market in having a licensing framework in place. SPUR members (BBC, FT, Sky News, Telegraph, Guardian) are building technical standards that may well become de facto requirements once legislation does arrive. Second: BBC Sport used a genuine 'digital first' approach at Milan Cortina 2026 Winter Olympics (SVG Europe) — 'serving audiences through social and online platforms as a priority rather than an afterthought.' This is the benchmark. Guardian Studios launching in 2026 is perfectly timed — but it needs a workflow that makes social-first publishing the path of least resistance, not an extra step.
actions: Monitor UK AI copyright consultation timeline — SPUR's technical standards may become legally significant once legislation arrives.
Guardian Studios should benchmark against BBC Sport's Milan Cortina digital-first playbook — key question: what workflow changes made social the primary publish destination?
Add AI governance section to MMR handover documentation: data ownership, editorial guardrails for AI use of assets — per ITV GenAI lead blueprint.

items.title: iconik changelog March 5, 2026: My Shares filtering (All/Shared with Me/Shared by Me) + favourite saved searches
iconik interface overhaul live from 4 March 2026: new nav, grey background, admin dropdown, Upload button removed — design system continues throughout 2026 (light/dark mode coming)
Backlight 2026 Iconik Media Stats Report — video = 64% of storage, audio +45% YoY, vertical publishing +120%, AI used 11.4M times, 903M assets, 324 PB total (BroadcastBeat, March 10)
LucidLink Connect GA (SiliconANGLE exclusive, March 5): mount S3 buckets as LucidLink Filespaces without data migration — byte-range streaming, no ingest, SOC 2 Type II + MPA TPN certified
LucidLink Connect — Blocks & Files deep-dive (March 10): TeamCache early access for office teams; API + SDK for custom orchestration; CLI linking in future release
LucidLink January 2026 release: Guest Access (browser-based external review, zero-knowledge security, no install required) + Directory Management API (programmatic folder/permissions automation)
NAB Show 2026 (April 18-22): Backlight booth N2829 — single-platform create/manage/publish; Harmonic AI orchestration; Avid AI workflow. Key vendor watch dates.
Guardian Studios formed Feb 26 2026: Leah Green (ex-Channel 4) as head — video-first, personality-led journalism mandate across multimedia teams globally
Guardian joins SPUR coalition (Feb 26): BBC, FT, Sky News, Telegraph — technical standards for AI use of publisher content; UK government AI copyright delay makes SPUR's early-mover position stronger
UK Government delays AI copyright rule change (VideoWeek, March 6) — kicks the can on legislation; SPUR coalition members are building de facto standards ahead of law
News Corp 'AI input company' after $150M Meta deal (March 4) — direct contrast with Guardian/SPUR ethical AI licensing approach
AWS Elemental Inference: AI real-time vertical video (9:16) from live feeds at 6-10s latency — saliency mapping, object/speaker tracking; Fox Sports + NBCUniversal live (Hollywood Reporter)
IAB UK: UK digital video ad spend £9.3bn in 2025, +20% YoY — now 24% of all UK digital ad spend; CTV = 34% of video; 57% of marketers expect higher video budgets in 2026

Buffer analysis: Instagram, LinkedIn, Threads engagement rates DOWN in 2025; TikTok and Facebook engagement UP — 52M posts analysed (CNET, March 9); March 2026 confirms Instagram thriving, TikTok volatile
YouTube CEO 2026 strategy: Shorts = discovery layer, long-form = depth/monetisation, creators = studios — integrated format system required
BBC Sport digital-first at Milan Cortina 2026 Winter Olympics: social/online platforms as publish priority, not afterthought — integrated linear and digital editorial (SVG Europe)
BBC iPlayer proposes opening to ITV/Channel 4 — platform consolidation signal; UK's most-used streaming platform may become PSB hub
BBC: AI-generated content surge — 'pipeline onto social media almost fully automated' (March 6); content authenticity is now a Guardian editorial competitive advantage

changes.summary: Tonight's standout new signal: AWS Elemental Inference — real-time AI vertical video from live feeds at 6-10s latency. Fox Sports and NBCUniversal already live. This is a direct infrastructure question for Guardian's live news and Guardian Studios: can you publish 9:16 vertical clips *during* a breaking news event? Pair that with IAB UK's data that UK digital video ad spend is now £9.3bn (+20% YoY, 24% of all digital), and the commercial case for Guardian Studios investment is irrefutable. Vendor update: LucidLink Connect now has multiple GA confirmations across SiliconANGLE and Blocks & Files — the S3 archive activation story is fully confirmed. No new iconik changelog items since March 5. BBC iPlayer consolidation proposal adds to the platform signals: UK PSBs are clustering around fewer, bigger destination platforms — Guardian Studios must be visible on all of them.
